methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Rondo Energy is a Series B climate and energy company founded by John O'Donnell and Eric Trusiewicz, having raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private firms and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including peers at Series C stage such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $150M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $285M. The peer-median method references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—KoBold Metals ($3.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—producing a median valuation of $1.0B and a range spanning $700M to $3.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $242M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $642M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around the appropriate peer set weighting; stage-progression methods yield more conservative outcomes than comparable company analysis on this peer cohort. Key sensitivities include the stage at which Rondo closes its next funding round and the trajectory of the peer set's public market performance, which currently anchors expectations in frontier-stage climate tech valuations.
